description: Profile restructuring — review migrations renaming columns and adding constraints on a 5.2M-row profiles table under zero-downtime blue-green deployment
review_checklist: Check for NOT NULL columns added without DEFAULT values on large tables
Verify indexes are created with CONCURRENTLY on large tables
Check if dropped or renamed columns are still referenced by application code
Check for lock-heavy operations on high-traffic tables
Consider the deployment strategy (rolling/blue-green) and downtime budget

ground_truth.description: Renaming username to handle breaks feed_service and search_service which still query profiles.username directly. In a blue-green deployment, old instances will fail immediately after the rename.
Changing display_name from VARCHAR(100) to VARCHAR(255) requires a full table rewrite with ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock on 5.2M rows, blocking all reads and writes.
Adding NOT NULL column is_verified without a DEFAULT on a 5.2M-row table causes a full table rewrite with ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock, blocking all reads and writes.

The column rename in migration_001 combined with a new index in migration_002 does not follow the expand-migrate-contract pattern. The old index gets silently renamed, creating a duplicate, while dependent services still reference the old column name.
